---
title: "Mathematics of Data Science"
author: "Xulin Pan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: bibliography.bib
link-citations: yes
fontsize: 11pt
geometry: margin=1in
---

# Preface {-}

This textbook is written for undergraduate students entering data science,
statistics, computer science, and related fields. Its purpose is to develop the
mathematical ideas that support modern data analysis, statistical modeling, and
machine learning.

The book emphasizes three themes:

1. **Representation**: how data are encoded as vectors, matrices, functions, and random variables.
2. **Uncertainty**: how probability and statistics help quantify variation and risk.
3. **Learning**: how optimization and modeling convert data into predictions and decisions.

The presentation is intentionally balanced. It is more rigorous than a purely
computational handbook, but more applied than a traditional pure mathematics
text. Every chapter contains definitions, examples, interpretation, and
exercises, together with code fragments in R.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)
options(width = 80)
```
